title: Tomato Galette
description: My Thermomix take on a Diana Henry-style tomato galette — a free-form tart with a buttery pastry. The Thermomix makes the dough.
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Diana Henry and the comforting appeal of tomato galette. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
steps: Place flour, butter and salt in the mixing bowl. Mix 8 sec / 0°C / speed 6 to crumbs.
Add cold water and mix 10 sec / 0°C / speed 5 to a dough. Wrap and chill 30 min / 0°C / no speed.
Roll into a rough circle, spread with cheese, layer tomatoes and fold the edges over 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
Brush with olive oil, scatter thyme and bake in a preheated oven 30 min / 200°C / no speed until golden. The oven does the baking step.
tips: Keep the butter cold for a flaky pastry.
Salt the tomatoes and pat dry so the base stays crisp.

faq.q: Can I make the pastry ahead?
faq.a: Yes — it keeps 2 days chilled or freezes well.

faq.q: Is this the original Diana Henry recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Diana Henry and tomato galette; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
